questions.text: What was Pharaoh's policy towards the Children of Israel at the time of Musa's birth?
questions.answer: He divided the people into sects, weakening one group by slaughtering their sons and letting their women live, acting as a corrupter.

questions.text: What was Allah's intention for the oppressed Children of Israel in Egypt?
questions.answer: To confer favor upon them, make them leaders, make them inheritors, and establish them in the land.

questions.text: What did Allah inspire the mother of Musa to do when she feared for him?
questions.answer: To nurse him, and then cast him into the river (Nile) in a chest, without fear or grief, promising to return him to her.

questions.text: Who picked Musa up from the river and why did Allah allow it?
questions.answer: The family of Pharaoh picked him up so that he might become an enemy and a cause of grief for them.

questions.text: What did the wife of Pharaoh say when they found the infant Musa?
questions.answer: 'A comfort to the eye for me and for you. Do not kill him; perhaps he may benefit us, or we may adopt him as a son.'

questions.text: What was the state of Musa's mother's heart after he was taken by Pharaoh's family?
questions.answer: Her heart became empty of all except him, and she almost disclosed his identity if Allah had not stilled her heart.

questions.text: How did Musa's sister help him while he was in Pharaoh's palace?
questions.answer: She watched him from a distance and suggested a household (their own mother) that could nurse and care for him.

questions.text: Why did Allah return Musa to his mother so quickly?
questions.answer: So that her eye might be comforted, she would not grieve, and she would know that the promise of Allah is true.

questions.text: What did Allah grant Musa when he reached full strength and maturity?
questions.answer: Wisdom and knowledge, as a reward for the doers of good.

questions.text: Describe the incident that led to Musa killing an Egyptian.
questions.answer: He found two men fighting, one from his own people and one from his enemy; he struck the enemy to help his companion, and the man died.

questions.text: What was Musa's immediate reaction after accidentally killing the man?
questions.answer: He said, 'This is from the work of Shaitan,' and prayed for forgiveness, which Allah granted.

questions.text: Why did Musa have to flee Egypt the next day?
questions.answer: The same man he helped the day before called for help again; Musa realized he was being exposed, and a man came running to warn him that the chiefs were plotting to kill him.

questions.text: What prayer did Musa make when he set out toward Madyan?
questions.answer: 'My Lord, save me from the wrongdoing people' and 'Perhaps my Lord will guide me to the sound way.'

questions.text: What act of kindness did Musa do when he reached the well of Madyan?
questions.answer: He watered the flocks for two women who were waiting because they were too modest to compete with the male shepherds.

questions.text: What was Musa's prayer after helping the two women in Madyan?
questions.answer: 'My Lord, indeed I am, for whatever good You would send down to me, in need.'

questions.text: How did one of the women invite Musa back to her father?
questions.answer: She came to him walking with shyness and said her father invited him to reward him for watering their flocks.

questions.text: What did the father in Madyan say to Musa after hearing his story?
questions.answer: 'Fear not. You have escaped from the wrongdoing people.'

questions.text: What qualities did the daughter mention when suggesting her father hire Musa?
questions.answer: Strength and trustworthiness.

questions.text: What was the marriage contract offered to Musa in Madyan?
questions.answer: The father offered one of his daughters in marriage if Musa would work for him for eight years, or ten if he wished.

questions.text: Where did Musa see the fire after completing his term and traveling with his family?
questions.answer: In the direction of Mount Tur.

questions.text: What was the first thing Allah said to Musa from the tree in the blessed spot?
questions.answer: 'O Musa, indeed I am Allah, Lord of the worlds.'

questions.text: What happened when Musa threw his staff as commanded?
questions.answer: It moved like a snake, and Musa turned to flee in fear, but Allah called him back to not be afraid.

questions.text: What was the second miracle Musa was given at Mount Tur?
questions.answer: His hand came out white from his garment without any disease.

questions.text: Why did Musa ask for Harun (Aaron) to accompany him to Pharaoh?
questions.answer: Because Harun was more eloquent in speech and could support him as a witness to the truth.

questions.text: What was Pharaoh's reaction to the clear signs brought by Musa?
questions.answer: He called it 'invented magic' and claimed he had never heard of such a thing from his forefathers.

questions.text: What did Pharaoh ask Haman to do to reach the God of Musa?
questions.answer: To build a high tower of baked clay so he could look at Musa's God, though he thought Musa was a liar.

questions.text: What happened to Pharaoh and his soldiers in the end?
questions.answer: Allah seized them and threw them into the sea because of their arrogance and wrongdoing.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the leaders of Pharaoh's people after their destruction?
questions.answer: As leaders who invite to the Fire, and on the Day of Resurrection, they will not be helped.

questions.text: What was given to Musa after the destruction of the former generations?
questions.answer: The Scripture (the Torah) as enlightenment, guidance, and mercy for mankind.

questions.text: Why does Allah mention that the Prophet was not present during Musa's story?
questions.answer: To emphasize that the information is coming through revelation, as a mercy from his Lord.

questions.text: What was the excuse of the people for not following the guidance before a messenger came?
questions.answer: They would have said, 'Our Lord, why did You not send to us a messenger so we could follow Your verses?'

questions.text: What did the disbelievers say when the Truth (Quran) came to them?
questions.answer: They asked why it wasn't like what was given to Musa, despite having disbelieved in Musa's signs before as well.

questions.text: How does Allah challenge those who claim previous scriptures are enough?
questions.answer: He tells the Prophet to ask them to bring a book from Allah that is better in guidance than the Quran and the Torah, if they are truthful.

questions.text: What does Allah say about the Prophet's ability to guide those he loves?
questions.answer: He cannot guide whom he likes, but Allah guides whom He wills, and He knows best who will be guided.

questions.text: What did the Quraysh fear would happen if they followed the guidance?
questions.answer: They said, 'If we follow the guidance with you, we would be swept from our land.'

questions.text: How does Allah respond to the fear of being swept from the land?
questions.answer: He reminds them that He established for them a safe sanctuary (Makkah) to which fruits of all things are brought.

questions.text: What happens to cities that are ungrateful for their means of livelihood?
questions.answer: Allah destroys them, and their dwellings become empty except for a little.

questions.text: Does Allah destroy any city before sending a messenger to its mother city?
questions.answer: No, He never destroys a city until He has sent a messenger to its main center to recite His verses.

questions.text: What is the difference between worldly enjoyment and what is with Allah?
questions.answer: Worldly enjoyment is temporary and a mere luxury, while what is with Allah is better and more lasting.

questions.text: What will happen to the false 'partners' of Allah on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: Allah will call them, but the partners will not respond, and those who were misled will admit their error.

questions.text: Does Allah create and choose whatever He wills?
questions.answer: Yes, 'Your Lord creates what He wills and chooses; not for them was the choice.'

questions.text: Does Allah know what is hidden in the breasts of people?
questions.answer: Yes, He knows what their breasts conceal and what they declare.

questions.text: What is the sign of Allah in the alternation of night and day?
questions.answer: If Allah made the night continuous until Judgment Day, no god could bring light; and if He made the day continuous, no god could bring night for rest.

questions.text: Who was Qarun and what was his relationship with his people?
questions.answer: He was from the people of Musa but he tyrannized them with his immense wealth.

questions.text: How massive was the wealth of Qarun described to be?
questions.answer: The keys to his treasure houses were a burden for a company of strong men to carry.

questions.text: What advice did the people give Qarun regarding his wealth?
questions.answer: 'Do not exult. Indeed, Allah does not like the exultant. But seek, through that which Allah has given you, the home of the Hereafter; and do not forget your share of the world.'

questions.text: What was Qarun's arrogant response regarding the source of his wealth?
questions.answer: He said, 'I was only given it because of knowledge I have.'

questions.text: What did the people who desired the life of this world say when they saw Qarun's splendor?
questions.answer: 'Oh, I wish we had the like of what Qarun was given. Indeed, he is one of great fortune.'

questions.text: What did the people of knowledge say to those who envied Qarun?
questions.answer: 'Woe to you! The reward of Allah is better for him who believes and does righteousness.'

questions.text: What was the final end of Qarun and his home?
questions.answer: Allah caused the earth to swallow him and his home, and he had no group to help him against Allah.

questions.text: What was the reaction of those who had envied Qarun the day before after he was swallowed?
questions.answer: They realized that Allah extends or restricts provision as He wills, and they thanked Allah for not letting them be swallowed too.

questions.text: To whom does Allah grant the 'Home of the Hereafter'?
questions.answer: To those who do not desire exaltedness (arrogance) upon the earth or corruption; and the best outcome is for the righteous.

questions.text: What is the rule of recompense for good and evil deeds?
questions.answer: Whoever comes with a good deed will have better than it; whoever comes with an evil deed will only be recompensed for what they did.

questions.text: What promise did Allah give the Prophet regarding his return to a 'place of return'?
questions.answer: That He who imposed the Quran upon him would surely take him back to a place of return (Makkah).

questions.text: Did the Prophet expect that the Scripture would be revealed to him?
questions.answer: No, it was only a mercy from his Lord; so he should never be an assistant to the disbelievers.

questions.text: What is the final command in the Surah regarding other gods?
questions.answer: 'And do not invoke with Allah another deity. There is no deity except Him. Everything will be destroyed except His Face.'

questions.text: What is the meaning of the name 'Al-Qasas'?
questions.answer: The Stories.

questions.text: What is the 'Blessed Spot' (Al-Buq'ah al-Mubarakah) in the story of Musa?
questions.answer: The specific place on the side of the mountain where Allah spoke to him.

questions.text: What is the 'Haman' character known for?
questions.answer: Being Pharaoh's advisor and the builder of the tower.

questions.text: What is the 'Safe Sanctuary' (Haramun Amin)?
questions.answer: The sacred area of Makkah protected by Allah.

questions.text: What is the 'Criterion' mentioned in the context of the Torah?
questions.answer: A standard to distinguish between truth and falsehood.

questions.text: What is the 'Mercy' (Rahmah) of Allah?
questions.answer: The divine favor and guidance sent through messengers and books.

questions.text: What is the 'Sign' in the swallowing of Qarun?
questions.answer: A warning against arrogance and trusting in wealth over Allah.

questions.text: What is the 'Sovereignty' (Hukm) mentioned at the end of the Surah?
questions.answer: The ultimate authority and judgment which belongs only to Allah.

questions.text: What is the 'Face' of Allah?
questions.answer: His essence and existence, which are eternal while all else perishes.

questions.text: Why was the mother of Musa told not to fear or grieve?
questions.answer: Because Allah promised to protect him and return him to her as a messenger.

questions.text: What is the 'Nile' river role?
questions.answer: The means through which Musa was carried into Pharaoh's household as a test.

questions.text: What is the 'adoption' Pharaoh's wife suggested?
questions.answer: Taking Musa as a son since they were impressed by him.

questions.text: What is the 'refusal of wet nurses'?
questions.answer: A miracle where Musa would not drink from anyone except his own mother.

questions.text: What is the 'Maturity' (Ashuddahu) of Musa?
questions.answer: The peak of his physical and mental strength when he received wisdom.

questions.text: What is the 'Work of Shaitan' Musa referred to?
questions.answer: The conflict and the accidental death that occurred.

questions.text: What is the 'Madyan' city?
questions.answer: The place where Musa found refuge and a family after leaving Egypt.

questions.text: What is the 'Strength' of Musa shown at the well?
questions.answer: His ability to water the flocks alone when others couldn't.

questions.text: What is the 'Trustworthiness' of Musa shown in Madyan?
questions.answer: His noble character and conduct with the women.

questions.text: What is the 'Eight or Ten years' contract?
questions.answer: The dowry or service Musa provided to marry the old man's daughter.

questions.text: What is the 'Baked clay' (Tin) used for?
questions.answer: To make bricks for Pharaoh's high tower.

questions.text: What is the 'Mother of cities' (Umm al-Qura)?
questions.answer: Makkah, the central point for guidance.

questions.text: What is the 'Luxury' (Atrafat) that leads to destruction?
questions.answer: Being blinded by wealth and neglecting Allah's commands.

questions.text: What is the 'Place of return' (Ma'ad)?
questions.answer: Makkah or the Hereafter.

questions.text: What is the final lesson of Al-Qasas?
questions.answer: That truth and righteousness prevail over tyranny and wealth, and all stories end with Allah's judgment.
